info.title: SATP - Asset Schema Architecture for Asset Exchanges
info.description:
SATP is a protocol operating between two gateways that conducts the transfer of a digital asset from one gateway to another. The protocol establishes a secure channel between the endpoints and implements a 2-phase commit to ensure the properties of transfer atomicity, consistency, isolation and durability.
The Asset Schema Architecture for Asset Exchanges defines the data structures and schemas used in the SATP protocol. It provides a standardized way to represent assets, their properties, and the transactions that involve them. It is designed to be flexible and extensible, allowing for the representation of a wide variety of asset types and their associated metadata.

This API defines the registry, asset schema authority and asset provider facing APIs.

**Additional Resources**:
- [Asset Schema Architecture for Asset Exchange](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-avrilionis-satp-asset-schema-architecture-06)
- [Asset Profiles for Asset Exchange draft](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-avrilionis-satp-asset-profiles/)

externalDocs.description: For more information about the SATP Gateway Client, refer to the official documentation.

post.description: This endpoint allows a client to register Token Issuance Authorization (TIA), signed by a given Asset Schema Authority (ASA).

post.description: This endpoint allows a client to send a token issuance authorization request (TIAR). The request includes the TIAR schema and an Asset Provider signature.

This endpoint allows a client to send an asset schema to be certified by the Asset Schema Authority. If accepted, the Asset Schema Authority digitally signs the asset schema, and includes it in the response.
